title: Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for satellite execs
summary: A shifting geopolitical landscape is creating significant business opportunities for satellite communications (satcoms) while also introducing new supply chain and regulatory challenges, according to industry executives at the Satellite Conference in Washington, D.C.

title: Moog taps Redwire to provide solar arrays for Meteor
summary: Redwire secured a $12.8 million contract to provide Extensible Low-Profile Solar Array (ELSA) wings for Moog's Meteor satellite bus, ordered by an undisclosed national security customer. The ELSA wings are designed to deliver 50% more power compared to previous models.

title: OrbitsIQ Global Announces Breakthrough in Space-Based IoT Connectivity
summary: OrbitsIQ Global (OIQ) announced a major breakthrough in space-based IoT connectivity, achieved through collaboration with unspecified partners. This advancement highlights OIQ's leadership in AI-driven secure connectivity for satellite and terrestrial networks.

title: From missions to systems: The architecture enabling a sustained lunar economy
summary: The article discusses a shift in focus from individual lunar missions to a broader systems-based architecture to enable a sustainable lunar economy. Industry leaders argue that the traditional framework of launches, landers, and national programs is outdated.

title: China’s Astronstone raises $29 million for reusable rocket with chopstick-style recovery
summary: China's Astronstone, a young launch startup, raised $29 million to develop its reusable AS-1 rocket, which features a chopstick-style recovery system. The funding will support the rocket's first flight.

title: Space Development Agency slows satellite launches to focus on on-orbit performance
summary: The Space Development Agency is delaying satellite launches to prioritize on-orbit performance, including checkout, orbit-raising, and network testing. The acting director emphasized that these steps are critical to the program's success.

title: NASA halts work on Gateway to develop a lunar base
summary: NASA has decided to pause its plans for the lunar Gateway in favor of prioritizing the development of a lunar base. This shift aims to accelerate efforts to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Space Force officer shares intelligence on threats to space systems
summary: A U.S. Space Force officer highlighted electronic warfare as an increasing threat to American space systems during a recent briefing. The discussion focused on technologies that could disrupt proliferated low-Earth orbit (PLEO) satellites.

title: SpaceEye-T: 25 cm Satellite Imagery and Tasking Now Available Through Pacific Geomatics Limited
summary: Canadian and international organizations can now access SpaceEye-T's 25 cm satellite imagery and tasking services through Pacific Geomatics Limited. This collaboration expands the availability of high-resolution Earth observation data.

title: SES orders 28 satellites from K2 Space for next-gen MEO network
summary: SES has placed an order for 28 satellites from K2 Space to support its next-generation MEO network, meoSphere, which is expected to be operational by 2030.

title: Finished Starfleet Academy? Boldly binge-watch the superb Star Trek: Strange New Worlds, just $2.99 a month thanks to this Paramount+ deal
summary: Paramount+ is offering a discounted streaming deal for Star Trek: Strange New Worlds, allowing viewers to watch the series for $2.99 a month. The show's fourth season is upcoming, appealing to fans of the original Star Trek series.

title: Amazon Spring Sale deals for stargazing 2026: Huge savings on telescopes, binoculars and cameras
summary: Amazon is hosting a Spring Sale in 2026 with significant discounts on telescopes, binoculars, and cameras for stargazing enthusiasts. This event offers substantial savings on high-quality skywatching optics.

title: Scientists discover mirror of our solar system in 2 exoplanets forming around a star
summary: Astronomers discovered two exoplanets forming around a distant infant star, providing insights into the early solar system. This finding acts as a time capsule to study planetary formation billions of years ago.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch Europe's 1st 2 'Celeste' navigation satellites early March 25
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch Europe's first two 'Celeste' navigation satellites on March 25. The launch can be watched live.

title: NASA's lunar Gateway space station is out. Moon bases are in
summary: NASA has decided to exclude the lunar Gateway space station from its Artemis missions and instead prioritize establishing bases on the Moon. This shift aims to accelerate human lunar landings.

title: A car-sized asteroid is heading toward Earth for a flyby later tonight: Here's what you need to know
summary: A car-sized asteroid discovered just days ago will make a close approach to Earth tonight. This marks one of the fastest-discovered near-Earth objects on record.

title: NASA's '1st nuclear powered interplanetary spacecraft' will send Skyfall helicopters to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA is set to launch its first nuclear-powered interplanetary spacecraft, Space Reactor-1 Freedom, in 2028. The mission will include sending a fleet of tiny helicopters to Mars.

title: Future Artemis missions could use fiber-optic cables to monitor moonquakes
summary: Future Artemis missions may utilize fiber-optic cables to detect moonquakes, providing a simpler method for studying lunar seismic activity and aiding mission planning.

title: Watch all your favorite sci-fi content like 'For All Mankind' and 'Star City' anywhere with this exclusive deal and get a free $50 Amazon Gift Card and 77% off NordVPN
summary: An exclusive deal offers discounts on NordVPN and an Amazon gift card for streaming sci-fi content like 'For All Mankind' and 'Star City'. The promotion includes up to $513 off, a $50 Amazon voucher, and four months free of NordVPN.

title: Hubble revisits the Crab Nebula after 25 years | Space photo of the day for March 24, 2026
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has released new images of the Crab Nebula, revisiting the iconic cosmic object 25 years after its first observation. The updated photos provide fresh insights into the nebula's structure and evolution.

title: Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu
summary: Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March caused severe flash flooding on several Hawaiian Islands, including Oʻahu. The Kona storms resulted in widespread damage and disruptions.

title: NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency
summary: NASA's Pilatus PC-12 aircraft, acquired in 2022, has been relocated to NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, to support flight research across the agency. The aircraft will continue aiding advanced technology development and research efforts.

title: Reminders of Where We’ve Been, Where We’re Going
summary: NASA announced transformative initiatives during a March 24, 2026, event featuring three Moon rocks on display. The initiatives aim to achieve the National Space Policy and advance American leadership in space, including plans to send astronauts to the lunar surface for the first time in over 50 years.

title: 3 Ways Students Can Get Involved With Artemis
summary: NASA's Artemis program invites students to participate in lunar exploration and Mars-bound technologies, offering opportunities during a new era of space innovation.

title: NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition
summary: NASA selected seven student teams as finalists in the 2026 Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, addressing aircraft maintenance challenges in the U.S. aviation industry.

title: NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova
summary: NASA's IXPE mission has captured new X-ray observations of the 2,000-year-old supernova RCW 86, enhancing the understanding of its structure and expansion. The data complements previous findings from the Chandra X-ray Observatory, revealing a large cavity influencing the supernova's development.

title: La NASA presenta iniciativas para cumplir con la política espacial nacional de Estados Unidos
summary: NASA unveiled transformative agency-wide initiatives during its 'Ignition' event to align with the U.S. National Space Policy under President Trump and strengthen American leadership in space.

title: NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets
summary: NASA is developing technologies to detect Earth-like exoplanets by analyzing reflected light, which may reveal signs of water and oxygen. This research aims to uncover potential habitable worlds beyond our solar system.

title: NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole
summary: NASA is contributing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA and ISRO to scout for ice on the Moon's south pole in support of future human exploration.

title: NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy
summary: NASA announced transformative initiatives to advance President Trump's National Space Policy during its 'Ignition' event. The agency emphasized its commitment to enhancing American leadership in space and fostering world-changing science and discovery.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The initiative was revealed by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is accelerating groundwork at Starbase in preparation for upcoming Block 3 Starship launches, demonstrating a focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from a repaired launch pad at Baikonur Cosmodrome. The mission follows repairs made after the previous Soyuz launch from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine expected missions, including flights by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced a new mission concept called NEO Hunter, aimed at enhancing Earth's planetary defense against near-Earth objects. The hybrid system combines various technologies to detect and potentially deflect hazardous asteroids.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans to send humans to the Moon and Mars, showcasing advancements at its Starfactory facility. The company is transforming steel production into spacecraft manufacturing for its Starship program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on unspecified space industry news. No specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, aiming to inform industry about upcoming activities and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. The innovation addresses the growing risks of disruption to critical satellite navigation systems.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: Contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft was lost due to an onboard anomaly, and teams are working to restore communication while investigating the root cause.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research and education in space technology.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has outlined a new plan to prioritize a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. This marks a significant shift in NASA's strategic priorities.

title: One-On-One With SDA’s GP Sandhoo
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused operations on its first operational satellite tranche due to minor issues, delaying troop benefits from improved communications technology. SDA Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo discussed the 'strategic pause' in a recent interview.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space introduced the Adagio hosted payload platform, enabling payloads to be attached to and detached from satellites in orbit. This model allows for flexible and reusable payload hosting in space.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys plan to launch a prototype orbital data center in 2027, joining the growing space-based data center industry.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission's digital infrastructure is crucial but often overlooked, marking a significant evolution from the Apollo era. NASA and Booz Allen are key players in developing this advanced digital architecture.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink, allowing customers to use both networks seamlessly. This move eliminates the need for customers to choose between providers for satellite internet access.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory battle at the FCC over their competing satellite mega-constellations. The dispute highlights the competitive and contentious nature of the satellite communications industry.

title: High Fire Volume Systems: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The Pentagon's Golden Dome program faces challenges with traditional space-based interceptors, leading to exploration of alternative approaches like deep magazine systems and directed energy. These alternatives aim to address engineering, procurement, and scaling issues.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new business segment called Satcat Terminal. This development is expected to benefit liberal arts majors.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing a renaissance with increased funding and startup activity. The country's small but growing ecosystem is gaining momentum.

title: Geopolitical Shakeups and Capital Wars Push Top Operators to Iterate Faster
summary: Geopolitical conflicts and rising concerns are highlighting the critical role of satellite communications, prompting top operators to accelerate innovation. Commercial satellite leaders emphasize the need for faster iteration in response to global tensions.

title: Launchers Cite Busy Manifests and Scarcity as Commercial Demand Grows
summary: Launch providers report extremely busy manifests over the next two years due to growing commercial demand, with SpaceX highlighted as a key player in the heavy-lift launch competition.

title: Lynk and SES CEOs Talk D2D Demand, As D2D Merger Awaits Completion
summary: The article discusses the growing demand for direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity, with SES CEO Adel Al-Saleh highlighting its potential despite market competition and geopolitical challenges. The merger between Lynk and SES is also mentioned as pending completion.

title: SDA Takes ‘Strategic Pause’ on Launches to Work Through Fixes
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused launches to address and fix issues, despite its mission to accelerate satellite production and disrupt traditional space acquisition. The pause aims to refine processes before resuming operations.

title: Astranis and Oman Group Execs Detail How Middle East Conflict is Driving Demand For Sovereign Satellite Services
summary: The Middle East conflict is increasing demand for sovereign satellite services, as highlighted by Astranis and MB Group executives during SATShow Week. The deal between Astranis and MB Group underscores the strategic importance of independent satellite communications in the region.

title: Redwire, Moog Sign Deal to Integrate Redwire ELSA Solar Array Wings with Moog Meteor Bus
summary: Redwire and Moog have signed a $12.8 million contract to integrate Redwire's ELSA solar array wings with Moog's Meteor satellite bus during SATShow Week.

title: Interview With Mini-Circuits CEO Jin Bains at SATShow Week
summary: The demand for radio frequency (RF) components is rising due to the increase in satellites in orbit. Mini-Circuits, led by CEO Jin Bains, is highlighted for its role in supporting satellite systems.

title: Amazon Leo Readies 200+ Satellites for Orbit as It Ramps Up Launch Schedule
summary: Amazon's Leo project has prepared over 200 satellites for launch, with six dispensers loaded at Cape Canaveral. The company is accelerating its orbital deployment schedule.

title: Space Force, Industry Leaders Warn of Critical Gaps in Orbital Intelligence
summary: The U.S. Space Force and industry leaders highlight significant gaps in orbital intelligence despite existing tracking systems like ground radars and GEO spy satellites. These vulnerabilities could hinder the ability to monitor space objects effectively.

title: Interview With TE Connectivity Principal Engineer Bruce Conway at SATShow Week
summary: TE Connectivity's Principal Engineer Bruce Conway discusses the company's interconnect solutions for satellites and solar panels, highlighting its heritage in deep space applications during SATShow Week.
